key_points: Three dorms torn down; WKU leasing Hyatt Place hotel as overflow housing
Rodes Harlin showers have broken ventilation, turning bathrooms into saunas
McCormack AC units are weak; bring a fan or suffer
key_quotes: My biggest complaint is on my floor there is no ventilation in the showers... turning the showers into a 24/7 sauna
WKU has leased the Hyatt Place just off campus to use as dorms... Imagine having your own dorm pool!
The ac units just aren't very strong (BRING A FAN)

key_points: Party scene is almost entirely Greek-controlled; non-members struggle to get in
Master Plan orientation is basically two weeks of early move-in and partying
Smash/fighting game tournaments, house shows, and niche clubs do exist
key_quotes: Party life is basically just the frats throwing. If you're a guy unless you're part of greek... have fun staying in your dorm
It's two weeks of partying. Hell ya it's worth it.
It honestly depends on may factors including your personal independence

key_points: Very little specific praise for campus food quality from any source
Flex Plan offers minor discounts but not worth it if rarely on campus
Local spots like India Oven and Double Dogs get more love than campus dining
key_quotes: Generally its just paying menu price for food + a 15% discount at some restaurants on campus
There are great restaurants in town
Love India Oven (literally my favorite)

key_points: No WKU-specific mental health support data available from student comments
Nursing program students report low confidence due to poor teaching methods
Kelly Autism Program exists but costs $5,000/semester, pricing out many students
key_quotes: I recently have been hearing about the decline in the nursing program at WKU... instructors don't give students what they need for success
I have high functioning autism... I can't afford it because it is $5,000 per semester
Have you spoken to vocational rehab, I believe they paid for it for my brother

key_points: Students report financial aid not covering costs, leaving families scrambling
National Merit Scholarship can dramatically change affordability at WKU
Medicaid/student loan policy changes hitting Kentucky students especially hard
key_quotes: After finding out how much money I'll be getting from the school... it's not near enough to not set my family back a bit
Online courses are a way of WKU increasing revenue
Only one Income Driven Repayment Plan for Federal Student Loans will be available moving forward

key_points: Nursing program reportedly overcrowded at 150 students with weak instruction
High volume of online courses frustrates students who moved for campus experience
RateMyProfessor is taken seriously here; students warn ignoring it is a mistake
key_quotes: They way overpack the class — like 150 students — and it's not uncommon for students to fail out
I felt silly to move up here from Tennessee to only go on campus twice a week... I feel like I did not learn enough
Check RateMyProfessor but read the reviews. I chose to ignore it my first semester and I'll never do it again

key_points: Dorm exception requests go unreviewed for over a month with no communication
Billing department responds within 24 hours if you call directly — email works too
TopNet and Blackboard login issues are common, especially for new students
key_quotes: I submitted my request around a month ago and it has not even been reviewed
I recommend emailing or calling the billing department directly... they responded within 24 hours and my refund followed soon after
Is it just me that is unable to login to my.wku.edu? I've tried on my laptop, personal phone, and work phone

key_points: PFT dorm to top of hill is an 18-20 minute walk — plan for early departures
Campus is heavily Republican; culture shock likely for Northeast students
Bowling Green is a small city; car helps for malls and off-campus errands
key_quotes: It takes about 18-20 min walk to go from the PFT dorms to top of hill
It is much more red than blue here. People are friendly, decent amount of night life
If you intend to have a job off campus that is not downtown, then yes [bring a car]

key_points: WKU dismissed as non-target school in competitive job markets outside Kentucky
Broadcasting/film program has appeal but degree value questioned vs. cost
Entrepreneurship and business programs seen as practically useful, not prestigious
key_quotes: Western Kentucky Central Community College isn't a great school
All of these fall in the 'effectively unknown/non-target' category
Your degree is what you make of it

key_points: Students paying full tuition but getting 3 of 5 classes online feel cheated
Off-campus housing near WKU runs $1,159/month plus utilities — not cheap
National Merit or honors scholarships dramatically improve value proposition
key_quotes: Lots of money to be attending there just to be on video calls
Rent is $1,159 plus utilities, however cable and internet are included
Money is a factor and unless I get a scholarship to UT I am leaning towards a cheaper school

key_points: Strong community loyalty; multiple generations of families have worked at WKU
Campus feels lively for sports and Greek life, quieter for everyone else
Friendly atmosphere but culturally conservative; outsiders may need adjustment time
key_quotes: My father worked for WKU Police, my mother worked for WKU Nursing, my grandmother worked in the DUC cafeteria. I now work at WKU too
People are friendly, decent amount of night life. If you're outdoorsy, it's a great area
I love everything about them except...

key_points: **Housing:** Three dorms demolished with no clear replacement plan for all students
**Nursing:** 150-student overcrowded classes producing under-confident graduates
**Academics:** Administration accused of pushing online courses to boost revenue, not learning
key_quotes: With the three newest dorms being torn down, it's a good possibility that WKU will lease a nearby apartment complex or hotel to overfill students
They way overpack the class — like 150 students — and it's not uncommon for students to fail out
Online courses are a way of WKU increasing revenue

key_points: Hyatt Place hotel leased as dorm gives some freshmen access to a pool
Study abroad program is active; students have gone to France, South Africa, Scotland
Fruit of the Loom outlet on Scottsville Rd sells cheap WKU gear nobody talks about
key_quotes: WKU has leased the Hyatt Place just off campus to use as dorms... Imagine having your own dorm pool!
I did two study abroads, one in France and another in South Africa. Both were life-changing
Fruit of the Loom brand store on Scottsville Rd next to the KFC
